heading: Rights and protections under Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938
label: 1313 Rights and protections under Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938

Application of Rights and Protections of Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to Congressional and Architect of the Capitol Employees, , , provided that:Pub. L. 101–157, § 8103 Stat. 944Nov. 17, 1989“(a)House Employees.—“(1).—In generalNot later than 180 days after the date the minimum wage rate prescribed by section 6(a)(1) of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 () is increased pursuant to the amendment made by section 2, the rights and protections under the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 ( et seq.) shall apply with respect to any employee in an employment position in the House of Representatives and to any employing authority of the House of Representatives.29 U.S.C. 206(a)(1)29 U.S.C. 201“(2).—AdministrationIn the administration of this subsection, the remedies and procedures under the Fair Employment Practices Resolution shall be applied. As used in this paragraph, the term “Fair Employment Practices Resolution” means House Resolution 558, One Hundredth Congress, agreed to , as continued in effect by House Resolution 15, One Hundred First Congress, agreed to .October 4, 1988January 3, 1989“(b).—Architect of the Capitol EmployeesNot later than 180 days after the date the minimum wage rate prescribed by section 6(a)(1) of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 () is increased pursuant to the amendment made by section 2, the rights and protections under the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 ( et seq.) shall apply with respect to individuals employed under the Office of the Architect of the Capitol.”29 U.S.C. 206(a)(1)29 U.S.C. 201

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The rights and protections established by subsections (a)(1) and (d) of section 6, section 7, section 12(c), and section 18D of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 ( (a)(1) and (d), 207, 212(c), 218d) shall apply to covered employees.29 U.S.C. 206

children.heading: Interns
children.label: 2 Interns
children.text: For the purposes of this section, the term “covered employee” does not include an intern as defined in regulations under subsection (c).

children.heading: Compensatory time
children.label: 3 Compensatory time
children.text: Except as provided in regulations under subsection (c)(3) and in subsection (c)(4), covered employees may not receive compensatory time in lieu of overtime compensation.

children.heading: Remedy
children.label: b Remedy
children.text: The remedy for a violation of subsection (a) shall be such remedy, including liquidated damages, as would be appropriate if awarded under section 16(b) of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 ().29 U.S.C. 216(b)

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Board shall, pursuant to , issue regulations to implement this section.section 1384 of this title

children.heading: Agency regulations
children.label: 2 Agency regulations
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (3), the regulations issued under paragraph (1) shall be the same as substantive regulations promulgated by the Secretary of Labor to implement the statutory provisions referred to in subsection (a) except insofar as the Board may determine, for good cause shown and stated together with the regulation, that a modification of such regulations would be more effective for the implementation of the rights and protections under this section.

children.heading: Irregular work schedules
children.label: 3 Irregular work schedules
children.text: The Board shall issue regulations for covered employees whose work schedules directly depend on the schedule of the House of Representatives or the Senate that shall be comparable to the provisions in the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 [ et seq.] that apply to employees who have irregular work schedules.29 U.S.C. 201

children.heading: Law enforcement
children.label: 4 Law enforcement
children.text: Law enforcement personnel of the Capitol Police who are subject to the exemption under section 7(k) of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 () may elect to receive compensatory time off in lieu of overtime compensation for hours worked in excess of the maximum for their work period.29 U.S.C. 207(k)

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: e Effective date
children.text: Subsections (a) and (b) shall be effective 1 year after .January 23, 1995
